assets.name: Bitcoin
assets.note: Strong institutional adoption. Regulatory clarity. Wyckoff spring zone
assets.note_detailed:
BTC holds Leader status in the framework, representing highest-conviction assets with established track records. Leaders receive priority for accumulation when conditions align.
As a store-of-value asset, the scoring heavily weights institutional adoption (40%) and supply dynamics (25%), with less emphasis on protocol revenue.

DIMENSION BREAKDOWN:
• Institutional (92/100, 40% weight): Excellent institutional presence. ETF flows, corporate treasuries, major custody support
• Regulatory (88/100, 15% weight): Clear regulatory standing. ETF approved, commodity classification in most jurisdictions
• Supply/On-Chain (85/100, 25% weight): Healthy on-chain metrics with favorable supply distribution and accumulation patterns.
• Revenue/Fees (15/100, 5% weight ⚠️ ESTIMATED): Limited fee revenue—protocol may rely on token incentives or is early-stage.
  (Score derived from LLM research — API data unavailable)
• Wyckoff (70/100, 15% weight): Currently in Phase B→C—historically favorable for position building as price structure suggests markup potential.

• Weekly RSI at 46.0 remains in healthy range.

This places the asset in the top tier of framework scoring, indicating strong alignment across weighted dimensions.

assets.name: Canton Network
assets.note_detailed:
CANTON holds Leader status in the framework, representing highest-conviction assets with established track records. Leaders receive priority for accumulation when conditions align.
As an infrastructure asset, institutional adoption (35%) and regulatory clarity (25%) are prioritized, reflecting enterprise deployment requirements.

DIMENSION BREAKDOWN:
• Institutional (85/100, 35% weight): Excellent institutional presence. Canton has exceptional institutional backing with DTCC tokenizing $100T+ in U.S. Treasuries, Northern Trust custody integration, and major participants including Goldman Sachs, BNP Paribas, Bank of America, Citadel Securities, and J.P. Morgan planning JPM Coin native issuance; BitGo custody is live and the network processes $350B daily in repo transactions via Broadridge.
• Regulatory (88/100, 25% weight): Clear regulatory standing. Canton has SEC no-action letter, compliance-first architecture with native KYC/AML, DTCC tokenized Treasuries partnership, and top-tier institutional validators (Visa, JPMorgan, Goldman Sachs). Purpose-built for regulated financial markets with explicit regulatory approval.
• Supply/On-Chain (75/100, 20% weight): Acceptable supply dynamics with some concentration or distribution concerns.
• Revenue/Fees (N/A, excluded): No protocol revenue data available for this asset type.
• Wyckoff (60/100, 10% weight): Currently in Accumulation Phase B—historically favorable for position building as price structure suggests markup potential.

assets.name: Lido DAO
assets.note_detailed:
LDO sits in the Observation tier, meaning it's being tracked for research purposes but doesn't yet warrant position sizing. The framework monitors for improving metrics.
As a DeFi protocol, revenue and fee generation dominates the scoring (35%), reflecting the importance of sustainable tokenomics in this sector.

DIMENSION BREAKDOWN:
• Institutional (55/100, 25% weight): Moderate institutional presence. Lido has meaningful institutional infrastructure including Grayscale LDO Trust (~$164k AUM), WisdomTree stETH ETP ($36M AUM in Europe), and VanEck's pending staked ETH ETF filing, but adoption remains nascent compared to BTC/ETH with small fund sizes and no major corporate treasury holdings.
• Regulatory (55/100, 20% weight): Uncertain regulatory standing. LDO faces mixed regulatory signals: the December 2024 Samuels v. Lido DAO ruling established DAO members may face personal liability as a general partnership, creating ongoing legal risk. However, the SEC's 2025 shift away from enforcement-driven regulation, Lido Labs' proactive SEC engagement, pending VanEck stETH ETF filing, and WisdomTree's European ETP launch indicate improving trajectory for liquid staking protocols.
• Supply/On-Chain (58/100, 15% weight): Supply metrics warrant caution—potential concentration or unfavorable distribution.
• Revenue/Fees (32/100, 35% weight): Limited fee revenue—protocol may rely on token incentives or is early-stage.
